prompt: Recommend initial layout and CONVENTIONS.md for a new Hono + Drizzle + Postgres TypeScript API that will be maintained mostly by Claude Code and Cursor.

assertions.text: layout.txt organizes by feature (auth/, billing/, users/) rather than primarily by horizontal layer (controllers/, services/, models/ as top-level)
layout.txt colocates test files next to source files (*.test.ts or *.spec.ts adjacent to source) rather than segregating them into a distant tests/ tree
layout.txt contains concrete domain directories like auth, billing, or users (not just generic scaffold)
CONVENTIONS.md explicitly addresses naming — e.g. avoiding generic names (Manager/Service/Handler) or requiring domain-specific names
CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline (a number, e.g. under 500 or 800 lines)
CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop (colocated tests, a single bash command, or similar)
CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns (reflection, runtime dispatch, heavy DI frameworks) — or equivalent — for agent-traceability reasons
CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior (grep-ability, context windows, str_replace, hallucination) rather than generic clean-code reasoning
